commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow repair can address the env-profile retention bug and misleading policy text before maintainers make the final provider-boundary decision.; Needs attention: The PR needs a security fix because forwarded env secrets can remain in a failure-retained sandbox.; Review finding: [P1] Clean env profiles before retaining failed sandboxes — internal/providers/opencomputer/backend.go:144-145 - [P3] Keep auth gu... (sha=cef7d5a13a3fd6021bdb075219f9d0ea8e1bb8e1)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an OpenComputer delegated-run provider using the `oc` CLI and a direct file API path for env forwarding, with config, docs, provider registration, archive sync, bridge handling, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `--allow-env` uploads a profile, skips cleanup while `shouldStop` is initially true, then `--keep-on-failure` can flip `shouldStop` false and preserve the sandbox after a failed command.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can address the env-profile retention bug and misleading policy text before maintainers make the final provider-boundary decision.

target.title: feat(provider): add OpenComputer sandbox provider
target.body:
## What

Adds `provider: opencomputer` (aliases `oc`, `open-computer`): a delegated-run provider backed by the [OpenComputer](https://app.opencomputer.dev) `oc` CLI (full Linux VMs). It follows the existing delegated-run pattern (`tensorlake`/`islo`/`modal`): Crabbox drives `oc sandbox create/get/list/kill` and `oc exec --wait` for lifecycle and command execution, while owning local config, repo claims, sync manifests/guardrails, slugs, timing summaries, and normalized list/status rendering.

## Design notes

- **Auth never touches Crabbox.** The OpenComputer API key lives in the `oc` CLI config (`oc config set api-key`); Crabbox stores/passes no `osb_` key in config or argv.
- **No env forwarding (by design).** `oc exec --wait` is a buffered request that cannot carry values off the command line, and the streaming exec mode does not reliably propagate exit codes — so there is no safe non-argv channel for forwarded env. `--allow-env`/`--env-from-profile` are **rejected before any sandbox is created**, so secrets never reach an `oc` invocation.
- **Archive sync without SSH/file-copy.** A `git ls-files`-driven gzipped tar is base64-encoded and appended to a remote staging file in chunks via `oc exec --wait`, then decoded + extracted in-sandbox. The provider advertises `FeatureArchiveSync`, so `--force-sync-large` and `--sync-only` are honored; `--checksum` is rejected.

## Direct-API / SDK transport — considered, not adopted

Per the maintainer suggestion to mirror wandb's API-client approach: there is **no official Go SDK** for OpenComputer (only TypeScript `@opencomputer/sdk` and Python `opencomputer-sdk`), and the `/api` REST surface (`X-API-Key`, `/exec/run`, `{path,content}`) is undocumented for Go. The supported, simple, e2e-proven `oc` CLI path is retained; a future REST transport could re-enable safe (off-argv) env forwarding.

## Review fixes (ClawSweeper PR #236)

- **[P1] secrets in argv** → env forwarding removed entirely; `--allow-env` now rejected up front. `env.go` / `writeRemoteFile` deleted.
- **[P2] `--force-sync-large` unusable** → spec now advertises `FeatureArchiveSync`; `--sync-only` supported; redundant in-backend reject dropped in favor of core's spec-aware gate; added focused coverage.

## Verification

`gofmt`, `go vet`, `go test -race`, `scripts/check-docs.sh` clean; core coverage gate 91% ≥ 90%.

**End-to-end against `app.opencomputer.dev` (redacted live output):**

~~~text
########## 1) DEFAULT SYNC RUN (archive over oc exec) ##########
leased ocbx_sb-f1ca9f48 slug=brisk-shrimp provider=opencomputer sandbox=sb-f1ca9f48 ...
sync candidate: 2 files, 25 B
sync complete in 4.517s
sync-marker-OK
nested-OK
opencomputer run summary sync=4.517s command=1.059s total=26.638s exit=0

########## 2) SYNC-ONLY (sync, no command) ##########
leased ocbx_sb-8898bba4 slug=pearl-shrimp provider=opencomputer sandbox=sb-8898bba4 ...
sync complete in 4.604s
synced /workspace/crabbox

########## 3) ENV FORWARDING REJECTED (SECRET_TOKEN set; nothing created) ##########
provider=opencomputer does not support forwarding environment variables: oc exec
cannot carry values off the command line, so secrets would land in argv. ...
# running sandboxes after rejection: []   (rejected before create; secret never reaches oc)
~~~

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zozo123
